name: Bakunawa
description: The Bakunawa is a serpentine dragon in Philippine mythology, believed to be the cause of eclipses. Described as a gigantic sea serpent with a mouth as big as a lake, a red tongue, whiskers, gills, and two sets of wings, its immense size allows it to swallow the moon or sun whole. Ancient Filipinos believed that the Bakunawa lived in the sea or the underworld and would rise to devour the celestial bodies. To prevent this, people would make loud noises, bang pots and pans, and play musical instruments in an effort to scare the Bakunawa away and force it to release its prey. Some stories depict it as having a beautiful upper half and a scaly, snakelike lower half. The Bakunawa's motivations vary between myths, ranging from simple hunger to a desire to possess the light of the celestial bodies. The creature's story reflects the ancient Filipinos' understanding of the cosmos and their attempts to control the unpredictable forces of nature. The myth also serves as a reminder of the constant struggle between light and darkness.
subtitle: Lunar Eclipse Serpent Dragon
appearance: A gigantic, serpentine sea dragon with a mouth as large as a lake, a red tongue, whiskers, gills, two sets of wings, and scales covering its body. Some accounts depict a beautiful upper half transitioning into a snakelike lower half.
habitat: The depths of the ocean and the underworld.
behavior: Driven by hunger or a desire for the celestial bodies' light, the Bakunawa rises from the sea to devour the moon or sun, causing eclipses. It is easily frightened by loud noises.

keyMyths.title: The Eclipse Eater
keyMyths.description: The Bakunawa rises from the sea to swallow the moon, causing an eclipse. Villagers create loud noises to scare it away, forcing it to release the moon.

keyMyths.title: Seven Moons
keyMyths.description: In one legend, Bathala created seven moons, but the Bakunawa swallowed six of them, enraging Bathala and causing an eternal chase.

keyMyths.title: The Enchanted Bamboo
keyMyths.description: A tale where the Bakunawa is tricked and trapped using enchanted bamboo, preventing it from swallowing the last moon.

sources.author: Maximo Ramos
